affected_groups: Teachers subject to redeployment under Act LII
Students affected by potential teacher shortages or strikes if mobilization occurs
content.decision_question: Employment security and professional autonomy versus administrative flexibility to reorganize the school network efficiently
content.evidence_boundary: Contested/unknown: whether redeployment under Act LII would in practice be implemented with or without consultation is a matter of administrative discretion and political choice, not yet settled by any pilot or legal precedent cited in the record; the scenario's own political-risk analysis flags the mobilization risk as plausible but does not quantify its likelihood or scale
content.tension: Forced or unconsulted teacher redeployment to the centralized site under the Status Law (Act LII) risks involuntary-displacement mobilization (as with the Tanítanék movement) and undermines employment security and professional continuity unless binding consultation, retraining and no-forced-transfer guarantees are legislated.
content.title: Decision tension A6: S2
value_poles: administrative flexibility to reorganize the school network efficiently
